debug_points.description: The road surface appears to be in good condition with intact asphalt. The paved street shows normal wear but no holes, depressions, or missing asphalt material. The road has clear lane markings and well-maintained edges.

The road surface shows general wear and aging with some surface irregularities and patching, but no actual potholes with missing asphalt are visible. The pavement has uneven texture and some repaired areas but no holes with exposed base material or broken edges.

The road surface appears to be in fair condition with wet asphalt visible. No potholes or holes with missing asphalt are detected. The gray/brown areas in the lower portions of multiple images are Street View stitching artifacts from the close camera angle, not water pooling or road damage.

This is a residential street in Panama City with asphalt road surface. The road appears to be in generally good condition with no visible potholes or holes with missing asphalt. There are parked vehicles along the roadside and the pavement shows normal wear but no structural damage.

The road surface appears to be in generally good condition. There is some surface wear and minor deterioration along the curb edges, particularly visible in images 4 and 6, but no actual holes with missing asphalt are present. The muddy/wet area visible in image 4 near the curb shows surface wear but no depth or exposed base material indicating a true pothole.

The road surface shows general wear and aging with some surface texture variation, but no actual potholes with missing asphalt are visible. The large gray/brown areas in multiple images are Street View stitching artifacts from the camera angle, not water pooling or road damage. The asphalt appears intact throughout all viewable angles.

The road surface shows general wear and aging with some rough texture, but no actual potholes with missing asphalt are visible. The large gray/brown areas in the lower portions of most images are Street View stitching artifacts (blurry, textureless smears) rather than road damage or water pooling. The asphalt appears intact throughout.

This is a residential street in Panama City with parked vehicles on both sides. The road surface shows general wear and aging with some rough texture, but no actual potholes with missing asphalt, exposed base material, or visible depth are present. The gray/brown blurred areas in the lower portions of several images are Street View stitching artifacts from the camera angle, not road damage.

The road surface visible across all angles shows worn asphalt with some discoloration and minor surface wear, but no actual potholes with missing asphalt, exposed base material, or visible depth. The parking area shows dirt/sandy patches between vehicles but these are unpaved surfaces, not damaged asphalt.

The images show a residential street with significant standing water/flooding covering much of the road surface, but no actual potholes are visible. The brown murky water obscures the pavement but there are no visible holes, broken edges, or exposed base material indicating missing asphalt.

The road surface shows general wear and some roughness but no actual potholes with missing asphalt are visible. The pavement appears aged with minor surface deterioration but maintains structural integrity. The greenish blur in several images is a Street View stitching artifact, not water or damage.

The road surface visible in the clearer images (East, Southeast, South views) shows well-maintained asphalt with intact lane markings, yellow-painted curbs, and paver stone sidewalks. No holes with missing asphalt, exposed base material, or broken edges are visible on the roadway.

The road surface visible in images 2-6 shows well-maintained asphalt with clear lane markings, yellow curbs, and proper drainage infrastructure. The muddy/waterlogged areas visible in images 1, 7, 8, and 9 are unpaved shoulders and drainage areas adjacent to the road, not actual road damage or potholes.

The road surface shows normal asphalt in fair condition with no visible potholes. The street is a residential area with parked vehicles on both sides. The pavement shows some wear and minor surface variations but no holes with missing asphalt, exposed base material, or broken edges are visible.

The road surface shows typical urban wear with some minor surface deterioration but no actual potholes visible. The pavement is intact asphalt with no holes, missing material, or exposed base layer. The gray-brown smears in the lower portions of most images are clearly Street View stitching artifacts from the camera's close/steep angles, not road damage or water pooling.

The road surface throughout all 9 images shows well-maintained asphalt with no visible potholes. The pavement appears intact with proper curbing, sidewalks, and drainage infrastructure. Some normal wear and minor surface texture variation is visible but no holes with missing asphalt or exposed base material.

This is a residential street intersection in Panama City. The road surface appears to be in good condition with intact asphalt. The large greenish-gray areas visible in multiple images are Street View stitching artifacts from the camera's steep downward angle, not water pooling or road damage.

The images show a residential street with significant standing water/flooding covering much of the road surface. The water is murky brown, making it impossible to see the actual road surface beneath. No actual potholes with visible missing asphalt, exposed base material, or broken edges can be confirmed - only standing water is visible.

The road surface appears wet throughout all images, creating a shiny/reflective appearance. The asphalt shows general wear and aging but no actual holes with missing material are visible. The gray/brown areas in the foreground of several images are Street View stitching artifacts from steep camera angles, not road damage.

The large gray-brown area visible in multiple images (particularly the Down view and North view) is a Street View stitching artifact - a blurry, textureless smear with no discernible pavement features, edges, or depth. This is not water pooling over a pothole, nor an actual road defect. The visible road surfaces in other angles show well-maintained asphalt with clear lane markings.

The road surface shows significant wear and deterioration with cracked concrete in the parking lot area visible in images 5-7, but no actual potholes with missing asphalt and exposed base material are visible. The parking area has weathered, uneven concrete with surface cracks and old patch repairs, but these are surface deterioration, not holes.

The road surface shows general wear and aging with some surface cracks and roughness, but no actual potholes with missing asphalt are visible. The large gray smear in multiple images (especially image 9) is a Street View stitching artifact from the camera angle, not standing water or road damage.

The road surface throughout all 9 images appears to be in fair condition with normal asphalt wear. No holes with missing asphalt, exposed base material, or broken edges are visible. The pavement shows typical urban road texture with some minor surface variations but no actual potholes.

This appears to be a residential street in Panama City with concrete sidewalks and driveways. The road surface shows general wear and aging but no actual potholes with missing asphalt are visible. Multiple drainage grates are present which are intentional infrastructure features.

The road surface shows permeable paver/grass-block parking areas adjacent to an asphalt street. The large gray-brown area visible in multiple images is a Street View stitching artifact (flat, textureless smear with no discernible pavement features), not standing water or a pothole. The actual asphalt road visible in images 4, 5, 7, and 8 appears to be in fair condition with minor surface wear but no visible holes or missing asphalt.

This is an indoor location - appears to be a children's play area or early childhood education center with marble flooring, activity stations, climbing wall panels, art easels, and various educational toys. No road surface is visible in any of the images.

This is a residential street intersection in what appears to be Panama City. The road surface shows wet asphalt but no actual potholes with missing material. The large olive-green/gray areas visible in multiple images are Street View stitching artifacts from the steep camera angle, not water pooling or road damage.

No potholes detected in any of the 9 images. The road surface is well-maintained asphalt with clear lane markings. The large gray/green smears visible in the lower portions of most images are Street View camera stitching artifacts from steep angles, not road damage or water pooling.

The images show a well-maintained urban street area with landscaped medians, grass strips, and sidewalks. The large gray-brown areas visible in multiple images are Street View stitching artifacts (blurry, textureless smears from steep camera angles) rather than actual road features or damage. No actual potholes with missing asphalt, exposed base material, or broken edges are visible.

No potholes detected in any of the 9 images. The road surface is asphalt in generally good condition with normal wear. The gray blurred areas visible in the lower portions of most images are Street View stitching artifacts from the camera angle, not road damage or water pooling.

The road surface appears to be asphalt in fair to moderate condition. There is some surface wear, uneven pavement transitions at curb cuts, and accumulated debris near edges, but no actual potholes with missing asphalt, exposed base material, or visible depth are present in any of the 9 images.

The road surface appears to be in generally good condition. The asphalt shows normal wear with some minor surface texture variations but no visible holes, missing asphalt, or exposed base material. The wet road surface and some discoloration are present but these do not indicate actual potholes.

The road surface shows a large area of standing water/flooding covering much of the street, but this appears to be surface water pooling on intact pavement rather than actual potholes. The visible pavement sections show permeable grass-pavers (eco-pavers) and concrete surfaces in fair condition without visible holes or missing material.

The road surface is paved asphalt in fair to good condition. There are some areas of surface wear and aging concrete/pavement on sidewalks and driveways, but no actual potholes with missing asphalt are visible. The drainage grates visible in images 5 and 6 are intentional infrastructure, not damage.

No potholes visible in any of the 9 images. The road surface shows typical urban asphalt in fair condition with some general wear. The large gray-brown areas visible in the lower portions of multiple images are Street View camera stitching artifacts (flat, textureless smears with no discernible pavement detail), not water pooling or damage.

The road surface appears to be in good condition with intact asphalt. The large gray/brown areas visible in the lower portions of multiple images are Street View camera stitching artifacts from the steep downward angle, not actual road damage or water pooling. The visible pavement shows normal wear but no holes with missing asphalt, exposed base material, or broken edges.

This location shows a well-maintained urban street in Panama City with paved roads, sidewalks, and grass medians. The road surface appears in good condition with no visible potholes. There is some minor wear and drainage channels along the curbs but no missing asphalt or actual holes.

This location shows a well-maintained commercial area with herringbone brick paving and asphalt roadway. The large gray/brown areas visible in multiple images are clearly Street View stitching artifacts (textureless smears with no discernible pavement detail), not water or road damage. The actual visible road surfaces show no potholes - the brick paving is intact, and the asphalt sections visible in images 4-8 show normal wear without holes or missing material.

The road surface appears to be in fair condition with wet asphalt visible in multiple images. The large greenish-gray blur in several images (North, Northwest, Down) is clearly a Street View stitching artifact from steep camera angles, not water pooling or road damage. Where the actual road surface is visible (Southeast, South, Southwest views), it shows intact asphalt with normal wear but no holes or missing material.

The road surface shows general wear and weathering, particularly visible in the northeast view where there's a deteriorated concrete/asphalt parking area with surface erosion and discoloration. However, no actual potholes with missing asphalt, exposed base material, or visible depth are present. The worn areas are surface degradation, not holes.

The road surface shows general wear and aging asphalt with some surface irregularities, but no actual potholes with missing asphalt or exposed base material are visible. The large gray/brown areas in the lower portions of multiple images are Street View stitching artifacts (flat, textureless smears without pavement detail), not water or holes.

No potholes detected at this location. The road surface shows normal wear patterns with some minor surface deterioration. The pavement appears intact without any holes where asphalt is physically missing.

This is an urban street in Panama City near commercial/office buildings. The road surface shows normal wear with asphalt in generally fair condition. The large gray-brown areas visible in multiple images are clearly Street View stitching artifacts (flat, textureless blurs with no pavement detail) rather than water pooling or road damage.

The images show a well-maintained commercial/hotel entrance area with herringbone brick paver driveway and standard asphalt road. The large brown/gray areas visible in multiple images are Street View stitching artifacts from steep camera angles - they appear as flat, textureless smears without any pavement detail, markings, or edges. No actual potholes with missing asphalt, exposed base material, or broken edges are visible anywhere in the viewable road surfaces.

This location shows a well-maintained parking area with interlocking brick/paver surface. The pavement is in good condition with no visible potholes, holes, or missing material. The gray blurred areas visible in multiple images are Street View camera stitching artifacts, not road damage or water pooling.

This is a commercial parking area with interlocking brick/paver surface. The large dark gray areas visible in multiple images are Street View stitching artifacts - textureless gray smears with no discernible pavement detail, not water or potholes. The actual paver surface visible shows good condition with no missing pavers, holes, or exposed base material.

No potholes visible in any of the 9 images. The road surface shows general wear and aging with some rough patches, but no actual holes where asphalt is missing. The large gray/brown blurred areas in the lower portions of most images are Street View stitching artifacts from the camera angle, not road damage or water pooling.

The road surface shows general wear and aging asphalt with some surface irregularities and a visible drainage grate, but no actual potholes with missing asphalt are visible. The brown/gray smears in the lower portions of most images are Street View camera stitching artifacts, not water or road damage.

This location shows a commercial parking area with interlocking brick/paver surface. The road surface shows normal wear patterns and wet areas from recent rain, but no actual potholes with missing material are visible. The gray smears in the lower portions of multiple images are Street View stitching artifacts from the camera angle, not road damage.
